summary: We are in a high-velocity execution phase, prioritizing SEO for Asia Star and Intelligent China, launching the Asia Start campaign with dual AI/traditional routes, and building toward a September joint report with IE Business School on Chinese companies going global.
summaryDetail: The most urgent priority this week is executing the Asia Start campaign with two parallel routes (AI-driven by Joseph, traditional by Daisy) and getting the Intelligent China webpage live. Simultaneously, the SEO team is auditing conversion tracking and drafting initial search visibility targets, with Asia Star and Intelligent China as top program priorities. The major initiative is the joint report with IE Business School, 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global,' targeting a mid-September launch at events in Singapore; a one-pager proposal and translation of the Chinese report are due imminently. Meanwhile, we are expediting social media agency selection, prioritizing Media Minds due to budget and timeline, and preparing for Professor Zhu Rui's book launch at EuroCham Shanghai. Upcoming: the China Going Global report work begins July 7, and we need to finalize the Intelligent China campaign route decision this week.

meetings.title: SEO Strategy and Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Discussion on improving search visibility for CKGSB programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs, and auditing current conversion tracking setup.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses jointly publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools and case studies, aiming for a September launch at events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and a new Intelligent China program launching in December, focusing on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: China-Europe Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on China-Europe relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; Asia Star page already live.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget constraints and time.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include a main research feature by Professor Leeway, op-eds from both deans, and industry case studies.
Target launch in mid-September at events in Singapore (Tech in Asia or Tyson).
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI-driven by Joseph and traditional by Daisy.
Intelligent China program targets foreigners, is in English, and focuses on module one (Beijing) initially.
Adopt editorial design direction for Knowledge revamp
Use Flow for design of China Going Global report instead of in-house
Invite all four agencies (Impactable, Media Minds Global, 麦炽, 省广) to participate in social media ad agency selection
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: Request for Proposal - social media ad agency 2026 - updated 0703

decisions.text: Proceed with EuroCham Shanghai event for Professor Zhu Rui's book launch
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: New book from Prof.Zhu Rui
